title: Halenia brevicornis
taxonomy: Halenia brevicornis belongs to the family Gentianaceae, a group of flowering plants commonly known as gentians. It is a species of flowering plant native to North America. Halenia brevicornis is classified as a herbaceous perennial plant, growing in a variety of habitats including mountains and alpine regions. This species is also known as a short-horned gentian due to its distinctive, short horns. The classification of Halenia brevicornis is further supported by its unique characteristics and growth patterns.

intro: Halenia brevicornis, also known as Mexican gold poppy or golden halenia, is a perennial plant native to Mexico, characterized by its bright yellow flowers and slender stems. The plant has been traditionally used for its medicinal properties, including its potential to lower blood pressure, improve cardiovascular health, and exhibit anti-inflammatory and antioxidant activities. The bioactive constituents of Halenia brevicornis include flavonoids, alkaloids, and iridoids, which are responsible for its therapeutic effects. In traditional herbal medicine, the plant is often prepared as a tea, tincture, or extract, and is used to treat conditions such as high blood pressure, anxiety, and respiratory issues.
